text: Let P,Q,R,S be attributes of a relation. Let X→Y indicate FD where X,Y⊆{P,Q,R,S}. Which is/are always true?
In the context of relational database normalization, which statement(s) is/are true?

options.A: It is always possible to obtain a dependency-preserving 3NF decomposition
options.B: It is always possible to obtain a dependency-preserving 1NF decomposition
options.C: It is not always possible to obtain a dependency-preserving BCNF decomposition
options.D: It is not always possible to obtain a dependency-preserving 2NF decomposition

text: Consider relations R(P,Q) and S(X,Y). Let E = {⟨u⟩ | ∃v∃w ⟨u,v⟩∈R ∧ ⟨v,w⟩∈S} be a tuple relational calculus expression. Which relational algebra expression is equivalent to E?
Consider a relation R(A,B,C,D). If {A,B} and {A,C} are the only two candidate keys, then the number of superkeys of R is ______. (answer in integer)
